purpose: Live worker output viewer rendering the agent's tool-use stream as a Claude-style conversation. Subscribes to the `worker-output` and `worker-complete` IPC events filtered by taskId, parses each chunk as line-delimited JSON (NDJSON stream-json), and dispatches events through processEvent: assistant text blocks accumulate into a single growing message (currentTextIdxRef merges sequential text deltas into one bubble); assistant tool_use blocks become collapsible ToolCallBlock entries (toolMapRef maps tool_use_id → message index so the matching user.tool_result event can be backfilled into the same block as ._result); user tool_result blocks attach to the parent tool call OR render as standalone if orphaned. Provides a Codex compatibility path: thread.started/turn.started are no-ops; item.completed of type agent_message accumulates text; on worker-complete, lastMessage is appended only if streaming text wasn't seen (sawCodexStreamTextRef gate). Toolbar Kill button calls api.killWorker(pid); Clear button wipes local message state.
gotchas: handleChunkRef pattern is critical — the IPC listener inside useEffect captures a STALE handleChunk closure if not redirected through ref. Without this, processEvent would reference initial state. ALWAYS update handleChunkRef.current = handleChunk on every render (line outside useEffect).

currentTextIdxRef is a numeric index into messages — if Clear is pressed mid-stream, messages becomes [] but currentTextIdxRef may still point to a non-existent index; the appendText guard `currentTextIdxRef.current < prev.length` covers this but flushText must be called after Clear to prevent cross-stream merging.

toolMapRef maps tool_use.id → message index. If the same tool_use_id arrives twice (shouldn't happen but possible with retries), the SECOND mapping overwrites and the first tool block becomes orphaned w.r.t. its result.
Codex 'item.completed' agent_message handling appends text and immediately flushText — preventing accumulation across items, which differs from Claude's stream where consecutive text blocks merge.

Codex worker-complete fallback: data.lastMessage is appended ONLY if sawCodexStreamTextRef.current is false. If Codex ever emits BOTH streaming text AND a final lastMessage, this gate prevents duplication; if Codex emits NEITHER, no text appears.
thread.started/turn.started are explicitly silenced — adding new Codex event types may need similar handling.
Auto-scroll on new messages always scrolls to bottom (no smart-scroll like LogsModal) — user reading earlier output gets yanked to bottom on every new chunk.
killWorker is sent the PID, not taskId — prop must be the actual OS pid, not the synthetic taskId.
JSON.parse failures fall back to appendText(trimmed + '\n') — non-JSON output is shown raw which is useful for stderr/debug logs but may be confusing for normal users.
patterns: Stream-JSON event router (processEvent switch) with provider parity (Claude SDK + Codex stream-json) — same parsing strategy used in PlanningModal.extractJSON.
Tool call + tool result correlation via id-to-index map — enables collapsed result expansion without parent re-render.
handleChunkRef.current = handleChunk pattern — workaround for IPC listener closure capturing stale state.
conventions: Class names: .claude-* prefix throughout (.claude-conversation, .claude-tool-call, .claude-message-text) — reflects original Claude-SDK styling that has been kept even when adapted for Codex.
Sub-components ToolCallBlock, ConversationItem inline in same file.
